concerns.analysis: The first page of the source PDF contains the 'Great Seal of the State of North Carolina' at the top center. In the HTML output, this image is missing and has been incorrectly rendered as plain text bracketed alt-text instead of an actual HTML image element. Since this is a primary government seal that only appears once in the document, its absence constitutes a missing meaningful image.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The Great Seal of the State of North Carolina located at the top center of the page.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: The output suffers from OCR left-boundary clipping errors in the final table on page 10. This alters the names of specific projects, changing 'Jordan Lake' to 'h Lake' and 'Mountain Island Reservoir' to 'untain Island Reservoir'. This alters the factual meaning of the text.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Jordan Lake - Folkners Branch
...
Mountain Island Reservoir
concerns.output_quote:
h Lake - Folkners Branch
...
untain Island Reservoir

concerns.analysis: The document contains a large, multi-page table starting on page 4. While the first section of the table on page 4 correctly identifies 7 columns, the continuation rows on pages 6 and 9 incorrectly split the '$' symbol into its own column. This causes the rows on these pages to have 9 columns (indices 0 to 8) instead of the correct 7 columns (indices 0 to 6). For instance, on page 6, column 4 contains '$', column 5 contains the numerical amount '800.00', column 6 contains '$', column 7 contains
concerns.source_pdf_quote: On page 6 of the PDF, the first table row has 7 columns, with the funding columns containing intact values like '$ 800.00'.
concerns.output_quote: The corresponding table row in the JSON has 9 columns due to the dollar sign being split: {"text": "$", "column_start": 4}, {"text": "800.00", "column_start": 5}, {"text": "$", "column_start": 6}, {"text": "800.00", "column_start": 7}, {"text": "lake-wide survey", "column_start": 8}.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (9): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio; 1; 2
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=9, pages_affected=9/10. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 1 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=1, examples=['mailto:Kevin.Hart@ncdenr.gov']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 2 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=2. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.
